methods.description: Quantitative analysis of planktonic foraminifers from sediment samples to determine abundance, species richness, diversity, and evenness, used for paleoclimate interpretation.

steps.description: Nine samples were examined for planktonic foraminifers at the Rushmere locality. Calcareous microfossils were dissolved in the uppermost sample at 510 cm, and only seven specimens, belonging to Globigerina bulloides and Neogloboquadrina acostaensis, both dissolution resistant taxa, were recovered.
steps.searchable_summary: Examine sediment samples for planktonic foraminifers and assess preservation, noting dissolution.
inputs: Sediment samples (9 from Rushmere locality)

steps.description: The remaining samples had a mean recovery of 212 specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: Recover planktonic foraminifer specimens from samples.
inputs: Processed sediment samples (7 samples with good preservation)

steps.searchable_summary: Count total number of planktonic foraminifer specimens per sample.
inputs: Recovered foraminifer specimens from each sample

steps.description: The number of planktonic foraminifers per gram increased upward through the Rushmere Member and peaked in the lower part of the Morgarts Beach Member (Table 2, Figure 5).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate foraminifer abundance normalized by sample weight.
inputs: Specimen count per sample
Original sample weight (implied)
outputs: Foraminifers per gram for each sample

steps.description: Samples were quantitatively dominated by G. bulloides, Globigerinoides obliquus, Gs. ruber, Trilobatus sacculifer, and Globigerinita gutinata.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and count individual specimens for each planktonic foraminifer species in each sample.
inputs: Recovered foraminifer specimens from each sample
outputs: Species list and count per species for each sample

steps.description: Species richness also increased upward through the Rushmere Member and peaked in the lower part of the Morgarts Beach Member, then decreased above 187 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the total number of distinct species found in each sample.
inputs: Species list and count per species for each sample
outputs: Species richness value for each sample

steps.description: Shannon Diversity (H') [55] and Evenness (E) [56] indices followed similar trends...
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the Shannon Diversity index for each sample.
inputs: Species list and count per species for each sample
outputs: Shannon Diversity index value for each sample

steps.description: Shannon Diversity (H') [55] and Evenness (E) [56] indices followed similar trends...
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the Evenness index for each sample.
inputs: Species list and count per species for each sample
Species richness for each sample (often needed for Evenness calculation)
outputs: Evenness index value for each sample

steps.description: Samples combined from the type locality exhibited a species richness of 25, though no sample had more than 17 species.
steps.searchable_summary: Aggregate species lists from multiple samples (e.g., from the type locality) to calculate combined species richness.
inputs: Species list and count per species for selected samples (e.g., from type locality)

steps.description: The number of planktonic foraminifers per gram increased upward through the Rushmere Member and peaked in the lower part of the Morgarts Beach Member (Table 2, Figure 5). Species richness also increased upward through the Rushmere Member and peaked in the lower part of the Morgarts Beach Member, then decreased above 187 cm. Shannon Diversity (H') [55] and Evenness (E) [56] indices followed similar trends...
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze trends of faunal metrics (abundance per gram, species richness, diversity, evenness) against stratigraphic position.
inputs: Foraminifers per gram for each sample
Species richness value for each sample
Shannon Diversity index value for each sample
Evenness index value for each sample
Stratigraphic position of each sample

steps.description: Samples were quantitatively dominated by G. bulloides, Globigerinoides obliquus, Gs. ruber, Trilobatus sacculifer, and Globigerinita gutinata.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify quantitatively dominant species based on counts.
inputs: Species list and count per species for each sample

steps.description: The overall Yorktown assemblage has closest affinities with the Low Latitude-Mid Latitude [55], Tropical-Transitional [18] and Warm [59] assemblages of the North Atlantic.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare the overall faunal assemblage characteristics to published assemblages for paleoclimate interpretation.
inputs: Overall species list
Relative abundances
Calculated metrics (richness, diversity)
Published descriptions of known assemblages
